_instructions: Captured during Phase A DOM exploration on 2026-04-19. Opus Clip's web app is a Next.js React SPA built on Radix UI primitives. No data-testid attributes exist anywhere — selectors rely on aria-label, role, data-state, text content, and href patterns.
_opus_version_notes: Radix UI primitives, Tailwind, no data-testids. URL-driven editor state via quickAmend query params.
_base_url_note: Scaffold originally said app.opus.pro — that does NOT resolve. Real subdomain is clip.opus.pro.

answered_questions.Q1_headless_safe: UNKNOWN — tested via Claude-in-Chrome (real browser) only. Recommend Claude-in-Chrome as default; gstack untested.
answered_questions.Q2_multi_platform_one_modal: YES. Publish on Social opens a single dialog with all 6 connected channels pre-listed. Per-platform copy is AI-pre-generated; per-platform tabs (Post/Reel/Feed/Video) are visible inline.
answered_questions.Q3_bulk_schedule_ui: YES — and this is the flagship finding. Click 'Bulk schedule' in clip library → enters select mode → 'Select all' → click 'Bulk schedule' again → opens mega-modal with every selected clip pre-scheduled on a default cadence (2-hour spacing). Final button is 'Schedule' (commit) or 'Close' (discard).
answered_questions.Q4_timezone: Opus account timezone. Confirmed: modal displayed 'America/Los_Angeles' for this user. Config's timezone key should match the user's Opus profile.
answered_questions.Q5_auto_post_queue: Bulk schedule IS effectively an auto-post queue — it auto-generates times for every selected clip. 'Customize time' lets you override individual slots. We didn't probe for other scheduling config depth.

dashboard.project_title_source: aria-label on the anchor (includes title + clip count, e.g. '$200/mo Everything ... 40')
dashboard.project_id_from_workflow_redirect: After processing starts, Opus redirects to /dashboard?projectId=P...&utm_...  — extract projectId query param

workflow_setup_page.entered_by: Drive picker Select OR successful local upload
workflow_setup_page.note: After clicking, Opus starts processing and redirects back to /dashboard with ?projectId=<P...> query param. Use that to locate the new project.

dashboard.upload_file_input: input[type=file] (hidden). accept includes video/mp4, video/quicktime, video/webm, video/x-matroska, mpeg, etc.
dashboard.upload_button_activation_note: ALL synthetic clicks (JS .click(), CDP mouseclick, CDP keydown Enter) fail Chrome's user-activation check for file chooser dialogs. Focus + real HID keypress (not CDP Enter) works, but Chrome rejects CDP-dispatched Enter too. Confirmed Phase C: file chooser never opens via any automated path — requires real human gesture.
dashboard.drive_picker_note: Clicking 'Google Drive' opens an in-page Google Picker iframe (docs.google.com/picker). The iframe is cross-origin — its DOM is not reachable from parent-page JS, and CDP clicks inside its viewport region are silently ignored by Google's isTrusted guards. Confirmed Phase C: user must click folder + file + Select manually in the picker.
dashboard.ingest_limitation_summary: Every ingest path requires exactly ONE real human click. Claude-in-Chrome automation can drive everything else post-ingest.
dashboard.recommended_ingest_path: Pre-upload the video to Google Drive via drive_upload.py (fully automated with our OAuth client), then have the user click through the Drive picker (folder → file → Select) — 3 clicks, all real gestures. Faster than native file chooser because no filesystem navigation.

drive_upload_prerequisites.note: To automate Drive upload (so skill only needs 3 user clicks for picker): requires a one-time OAuth 2.0 client in the user's GCP project + rclone-style OAuth flow. Setup captured in Phase C (2026-04-19).
drive_upload_prerequisites.service_account_created: opus-clips-uploader@gen-lang-client-0527845499.iam.gserviceaccount.com  — useful for Drive folder write perms but CANNOT OWN files on personal Gmail (no storage quota). Left as SA for now.
drive_upload_prerequisites.oauth_client_type: Desktop app OAuth 2.0 Client ID in the user's GCP project — REQUIRED because rclone's default shared-project hits 'Queries per minute' rate limit at 99%+ for a 290MB upload.
drive_upload_prerequisites.oauth_token_storage: Not persisted to disk by default (safety system blocks config writes). For permanent skill use, user should explicitly approve rclone config at ~/.config/rclone/rclone.conf.
drive_upload_prerequisites.upload_helper_script: opus-clips/drive_upload.py  — uses Python google-api-python-client with a scoped token; handles resumable upload in 8MB chunks with automatic retry.

per_clip_action_buttons_by_text.enhance_speech: button with textContent === 'Enhance speech' (one per clip card)

clip_library.per_clip_action_iteration_note: These buttons repeat per clip card. To target clip N, select the Nth element of the button query. Cards render as a vertical list in default view.
clip_library.bulk_select_entry: button with textContent === 'Select'  (also triggered by clicking 'Bulk schedule' from the single-clip Publish modal)
clip_library.trending_badge_text: '🔥 Trending' appears next to good clips; use as a proxy for viral score ≥ threshold until we find the numeric score.

bulk_selection_mode.entered_by: Clicking 'Bulk schedule' from the per-clip Publish modal, OR likely via 'Select' button on the library top bar
bulk_selection_mode.note: In this mode, each clip card has a checkbox in its corner. Click the card (or checkbox) to toggle. 'Select all' checks every clip at once.

clip_editor.url_pattern_minimum: https://clip.opus.pro/editor-ux/{projectId}.{clipHash}?clipId={clipHash}&clipRank={N}  (editType/quickAmend optional — AI enhance panel can drive changes live)

ai_enhance_panel.open_trigger: button with textContent === 'AI enhance' in the right sidebar
ai_enhance_panel.panel_open_indicator: after click, a flyout appears left of the sidebar with header 'AI enhance' and rows for Remove filler words / Remove pauses / etc.
ai_enhance_panel.direct_action_note: These rows are one-click actions — clicking immediately mutates the clip in place. Remove filler words may no-op on clips with no detected fillers (Phase C test clip 'Fail Better' 58s had none, duration unchanged). URL does NOT change — no quickAmend gets appended. To commit, click 'Save changes' in the top bar afterwards.

subpanel_elements.pause_duration_slider: input[type="range"] or a slider control labeled 'Pause duration' (default 0s)
subpanel_elements.detection_text_regex: /(\d+)\s+pauses? longer than (\d+\.?\d*)s are detected\.\s+(\d+\.?\d*)s can be removed from your video\./

Remove pauses.flow: Click 'Remove pauses' in AI enhance panel → subpanel opens with slider + 'Remove (N)' button → optionally adjust slider to change threshold → click 'Remove (N)' to apply → duration shrinks by reported seconds.
Remove pauses.phase_c_evidence: Test clip 'Fail Better' (Apr 2026 run, project P30420004wHK clip #1): 6 pauses detected, 6.3s removable. After Remove(6) click: duration went 00:58.14 → 00:52.07 (delta 6.07s, matches detection).

toggle_switches.targeting_pattern: Find the row by its label textContent, then document.querySelectorAll('button[role="switch"]')[N] — switches render in the order listed above. Default observed: AI keywords highlighter ON, others OFF.

enhancer_config_mapping.config.enhancer_settings.remove_filler_w: click button with textContent === 'Remove filler words' ONCE per session (direct-action; verify by duration shrink or transcript diff; may no-op if no fillers detected)
enhancer_config_mapping.config.enhancer_settings.remove_silences: click button 'Remove pauses' → wait for subpanel → click button matching /^Remove\s*\(\d+\)$/ in subpanel → duration shrinks

clip_editor.automation_strategy: For every approved clip: (a) open editor URL, (b) wait for right sidebar 'AI enhance' button to render, (c) click it to open panel, (d) click 'Remove filler words' and 'Remove pauses' (one-shot actions), (e) click 'Save changes' top-right. No per-toggle state check needed for filler/pauses. Also supports URL-driven shortcut: editType=voiceEnhancement&quickAmend=... from clicking 'Enhance speech' on the library card — unclear whether that equals filler+pauses or something else.

single_post_schedule_modal.per_platform_post_type_tabs: button[role="tab"] — e.g. Post/Reel for IG, Feed/Inbox for FB, Video for LI, Video for TikTok, YouTube fixed
single_post_schedule_modal.bulk_schedule_escape_button: button with textContent === 'Bulk schedule' inside this modal — exits the single-post flow and enters bulk-select mode (!)
single_post_schedule_modal.close_button: button with textContent === 'Close' (may need Escape key as fallback — Close didn't always dismiss cleanly in our test)

bulk_schedule_modal.opened_by: Bulk-select mode → click 'Bulk schedule' (top-level action bar)
bulk_schedule_modal.default_cadence_observed: Clips spaced ~2 hours apart, starting the day after scheduling. e.g. 1:00 AM, 3:00 AM, 5:00 AM, 7:00 AM, 9:00 AM ...

sidebar_nav.notes: Each sidebar item is a <div> (not <a>) with an aria-label. Navigation likely via JS click.
